title: Living in Boulder vlog 5 - 17 - 2018: Google Earthing BouldeRome

enrichment.summary: Kyle describes his ongoing Google Earth project mapping Boulder County, Colorado onto ancient Latium (Rome), finding geographic and historical parallels between the two regions. He details technical frustrations with Google Earth crashing, his method of matching 30 Boulder mountain towns to 30 Latium city-states, and his theory that Boulder County and Latium are nearly identical in size (~1860 square kilometers).
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Genuinely excited by the geographic and historical parallels; sees it as a meaningful pattern, not coincidence
Favorite month because of how green everything is
Getting really good with history — timelines for everything are impressive
Frustrating to work with; crashes constantly and loses progress

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My light bulb died right when I hit play on the camera.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's May 17, 2018, at 5:44 PM, and it's super green outside.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: May is my favorite month because it's so green.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I thought I did a video after 'Boulder photos,' but I guess not.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've moved on from the 'Boulder photos' project.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There are a couple more photos of ditches in Boulder I need to address.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn't know exactly where some of the Boulder Canyon photos were taken.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I made a folder with 175 photos of Boulder, not including Carbondale.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Carbondale is the only mountain town with its own song folder, about 50 songs.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I decided to work on my Google Earth project instead of finishing the photos.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have three giant Google Earth files open simultaneously, cutting and pasting between them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My Google Earth crashed about 10 times yesterday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Right-click paste crashes Google Earth; I have to double-click and highlight first.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had to redo work multiple times because of crashes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I decided to finish mapping Boulder County and Boulder Row in Google Earth.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I found a map and a website with GPS coordinates and names of all the mines in Boulder County.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The map I'm using is from 1914, and my Boulder map is from 1915, so they align well.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I traced the Switzerland Trail and all the towns in Boulder County.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I created 30 Italian towns in my Google Earth project to match 30 Boulder towns.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm building territories around each of these 30 mountain villages in Boulder County.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Ancient Latium had 30 city-states, which I'm replicating in my project.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Greece had 12 as a magical number, but Latium used 30.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Boulder County is like a mini-America with 30 little states.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Wikipedia says the size of ancient Latium was similar to Boulder County.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Boulder County is about 1,893 square kilometers, almost matching ancient Latium.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Caribou in Boulder County is like Alba Longa in Rome, both former capitals that were abandoned.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There's a place called Albeon in Boulder County, matching a town in ancient Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Albeon is an ancient Native American site, possibly the oldest archaeological city in America.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Some towns in Boulder County have disappeared, just like in ancient Latium.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm building 30 evenly spaced city-states in Boulder County, mirroring ancient Latium.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm excited to see how this project turns out, especially when high.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have to remember not to right-click paste because it crashes Google Earth.
